title: carex vulpinoidea var. microsperma
intro: Carex vulpinoidea var. microsperma, also known as the lesser woodland sedge, is a perennial herb native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting respiratory health. Carex vulpinoidea var. microsperma can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
